title: OEE & Downtime Calculator
description: Calculates Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) based on Availability, Performance, and Quality, with detailed downtime loss analysis and actionable improvement suggestions. Compliant with ISO 22400, Lean Manufacturing, and Six Sigma standards.

inputs.label: Planned Production Time (minutes)
inputs.businessContext: Total scheduled time for production in a shift/day (e.g., 8 hours = 480 minutes).

inputs.label: Total Downtime (minutes)
inputs.businessContext: Sum of all unplanned and planned stops (breakdowns, setup, maintenance).

inputs.label: Ideal Cycle Time (minutes per unit)
inputs.businessContext: Theoretical fastest time to produce one unit under optimal conditions.

inputs.label: Total Parts Produced
inputs.businessContext: Total count of units produced during the planned production time.

inputs.label: Defective Parts
inputs.businessContext: Number of units that do not meet quality standards (scrap, rework).

inputs.label: Shift Type
inputs.businessContext: Type of shift to adjust for fatigue or environmental factors.

inputs.label: Include Micro-Stops (< 2 min) in Downtime
inputs.businessContext: If true, small stops are counted as downtime per Six Sigma guidelines.

rules.message: Downtime cannot exceed planned production time.
Defective parts must be less than or equal to total parts produced.
Ideal cycle time must be greater than zero.

thresholds.message: Quality below 99% – defect rate too high.

formulas.label: Operating Time
formulas.description: Time actually available for production after deducting downtime.

formulas.label: Availability
formulas.description: Ratio of operating time to planned production time.

formulas.label: Net Operating Time
formulas.description: Theoretical time required to produce total parts at ideal cycle time.

formulas.label: Performance
formulas.description: Ratio of net operating time to actual operating time (speed efficiency).

formulas.label: Quality
formulas.description: Ratio of good parts to total parts produced.

formulas.label: Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE)
formulas.description: Primary metric combining availability, performance, and quality.

primary.label: Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE)
primary.description: Primary result calculated as Availability × Performance × Quality.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Speed Loss
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Loss due to running slower than ideal cycle time.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Downtime Loss
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Loss due to planned and unplanned stops.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Quality Loss
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Loss due to defective parts (scrap and rework).

suggestedActions.action: Implement Total Productive Maintenance (TPM) and SMED to reduce setup and breakdown times.
Analyze cycle time variances, reduce micro-stops, and standardize operator methods.
Apply Six Sigma DMAIC to identify root causes of defects and implement poka-yoke (mistake-proofing).
Conduct a full OEE improvement kaizen event focusing on all three loss categories.

dataConfidenceAdjusted.label: Data Confidence Adjusted OEE
dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: OEE adjusted for data quality (e.g., manual entry errors, sensor accuracy). Uses a confidence factor of 0.95 by default.
